title: achillea millefolium subsp. ceretanica
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. ceretanica, also known as Ceretan Yarrow, is a subspecies of the Yarrow plant, a flowering perennial native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting immune function, while also exhibiting potential anti-cancer and antioxidant activities. Achillea millefolium subsp. ceretanica can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for its herbal benefits, although its preparations and uses may vary depending on the region and cultural practices.
